story.soThat: I can trace API requests and monitor application performance without manual logging in every endpoint
story.tasks:
- Task 1: Create LoggingInterceptor with request logging (AC: 7.4.1, 7.4.2) - Create `src/common/interceptors/logging.interceptor.ts` - Implement `NestInterceptor` interface with `intercept()` method - Extract request details (method, URL, user agent) from ExecutionContext - Log request start with timestamp and request details
- Task 2: Implement X-Request-ID header handling (AC: 7.4.5) - Check for existing `X-Request-ID` header in request - Generate UUID v4 if request ID not present - Store request ID in request object for access throughout lifecycle - Add `X-Request-ID` header to response
- Task 3: Implement response logging with duration calculation (AC: 7.4.3, 7.4.6) - Record request start timestamp - Use RxJS `tap()` operator to intercept response - Calculate duration (end timestamp - start timestamp) - Extract response status code from response object - Log response with status code, duration, request ID
- Task 4: Implement conditional log levels based on status code (AC: 7.4.3) - Use `info` level for 2xx and 3xx status codes - Use `warn` level for 4xx status codes (client errors) - Use `error` level for 5xx status codes (server errors)
- Task 5: Implement sensitive data scrubbing (AC: 7.4.4) - Create sanitization utility for request body - Detect and exclude sensitive fields (password, token, secret, apiKey, creditCard, authorization) - Apply sanitization to request body before logging - Do NOT log response body (only status code and duration)
- Task 6: Register interceptor globally in main.ts (AC: 7.4.1) - Import LoggingInterceptor in `main.ts` - Register with `app.useGlobalInterceptors(new LoggingInterceptor(logger))` - Ensure interceptor runs after JwtAuthGuard and PermissionsGuard
- Task 7: Write comprehensive tests (AC: all) - Unit tests for LoggingInterceptor - Test request logging with all required fields - Test response logging with duration calculation - Test X-Request-ID generation and propagation - Test sensitive data scrubbing - Test conditional log levels based on status codes - Integration tests with actual HTTP requests - E2E tests for full request/response lifecycle
- Task 8: Documentation and verification (AC: all) - Verify request logs appear in console/file - Verify response logs include correct duration - Verify X-Request-ID header in response - Test with various endpoints and status codes - Verify sensitive data NOT present in logs

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
AC-7.4.1: LoggingInterceptor created and registered globally - Created in `src/common/interceptors/logging.interceptor.ts` - Registered globally in `main.ts` with `app.useGlobalInterceptors()` - Implements NestJS `NestInterceptor` interface
AC-7.4.2: Each request logged with: method, URL, user agent, request ID, timestamp - HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.) - Full URL path with query parameters - User-Agent header value - Request ID (UUID) - Timestamp in ISO 8601 format (UTC) - Log level: `info`
AC-7.4.3: Each response logged with: status code, duration (ms), timestamp - HTTP status code (200, 201, 400, 500, etc.) - Request duration in milliseconds (calculated from request start to response end) - Timestamp in ISO 8601 format (UTC) - Log level: `info` for successful responses (2xx, 3xx), `warn` for client errors (4xx), `error` for server errors (5xx)
AC-7.4.4: Sensitive data excluded from request/response logs (passwords, tokens in body) - Request body sanitized before logging - Sensitive fields excluded: password, token, secret, apiKey, creditCard, authorization, accessToken, refreshToken - Response body NOT logged by default (to avoid sensitive data leakage) - Only status code and duration logged for responses
AC-7.4.5: X-Request-ID header support: Generate UUID if not present, propagate if exists - Check incoming request for `X-Request-ID` header - If present: Use existing request ID (propagate from client/upstream service) - If missing: Generate new UUID v4 request ID - Add `X-Request-ID` header to response - Include request ID in all log entries for correlation
AC-7.4.6: Request ID included in all log entries for correlation - Request log includes: `requestId` in context - Response log includes: `requestId` in context - Enables correlation of request/response pairs in log aggregation systems - Request ID available throughout request lifecycle via request object

artifacts.docs:
- path: docs/tech-spec-epic-7.md title: Epic Technical Specification: Developer Infrastructure section: Story 7.4 - Request/Response Logging Interceptor snippet: Defines LoggingInterceptor as global interceptor that logs request (method, URL, user agent, requestId) and response (status code, duration). Integrates with Winston logger from Story 7.3. X-Request-ID header support for distributed tracing. Sensitive data scrubbing required.
- path: docs/tech-spec-epic-7.md title: Epic Technical Specification: Developer Infrastructure section: Workflows and Sequencing - Request Lifecycle snippet: Request lifecycle flow: LoggingInterceptor → JwtAuthGuard → PermissionsGuard → ValidationPipe → Controller → LoggingInterceptor (response). Interceptors execute BEFORE and AFTER controller using RxJS operators (tap, catchError).
- path: docs/tech-spec-epic-7.md title: Epic Technical Specification: Developer Infrastructure section: Data Models and Contracts - LogEntry Interface snippet: LogEntry interface defines structured log format with timestamp (ISO 8601 UTC), level (debug/info/warn/error), message, context (module, method, requestId, userId, domainId, duration, statusCode), and optional stack trace.
- path: docs/tech-spec-epic-7.md title: Epic Technical Specification: Developer Infrastructure section: Performance - Interceptor Performance snippet: Interceptor overhead must be < 5ms per request (p95). Duration calculation using high-precision timestamps (process.hrtime.bigint). Non-blocking logging operations (async). Zero impact on request throughput.
- path: docs/tech-spec-epic-7.md title: Epic Technical Specification: Developer Infrastructure section: Security - Sensitive Data Protection snippet: Request body MUST be sanitized before logging (GDPR, PCI compliance). Sensitive fields: password, token, secret, apiKey, creditCard, authorization, accessToken, refreshToken. Response body NOT logged by default. Reuse sanitization from Winston logger.
- path: docs/tech-spec-epic-7.md title: Epic Technical Specification: Developer Infrastructure section: Acceptance Criteria - AC-7.4.1 through AC-7.4.6 snippet: Complete acceptance criteria definitions including LoggingInterceptor creation, request/response logging fields, X-Request-ID header support, sensitive data exclusion, and request ID correlation.
- path: stories/7-3-structured-logging-winston.md title: Story 7.3: Structured Logging (Winston) section: Dev Notes - Winston Logger Service snippet: LoggerService injectable from src/common/logger/logger.service.ts. Methods: log(), info(), warn(), error(), debug(). Accepts context object with metadata (module, method, requestId, userId, domainId). JSON structured format in production, pretty-print in development.
- path: stories/7-3-structured-logging-winston.md title: Story 7.3: Structured Logging (Winston) section: Project Structure Notes - Sensitive Data Scrubbing snippet: sanitizeContext() function in src/config/logger.config.ts detects sensitive fields (password, token, secret, apiKey, creditCard, authorization, accessToken, refreshToken) and replaces with '[REDACTED]'. Recursive sanitization for nested objects. Pattern can be reused for request body sanitization.
artifacts.code:
- path: src/common/logger/logger.service.ts kind: service symbol: LoggerService lines: 1-68 reason: Injectable logger service to be used by LoggingInterceptor. Provides log(), info(), warn(), error(), debug() methods with context support. Already configured and available from Story 7.3.
- path: src/config/logger.config.ts kind: configuration symbol: sanitizeContext lines: 45-101 reason: Sanitization function that removes sensitive data from context objects. Can be adapted for request body sanitization in LoggingInterceptor. Handles nested objects, arrays, and circular references.
- path: src/config/logger.config.ts kind: configuration symbol: SENSITIVE_FIELDS lines: 27-36 reason: List of sensitive field names to exclude from logs (password, token, secret, apiKey, creditCard, authorization, accessToken, refreshToken). Use same list for request body sanitization.
- path: src/config/logger.config.ts kind: configuration symbol: LogEntry lines: 8-21 reason: Interface defining log entry structure with timestamp, level, message, context (module, method, requestId, userId, domainId), and optional stack trace. LoggingInterceptor should follow this format.
- path: src/main.ts kind: bootstrap symbol: bootstrap lines: 7-41 reason: Application bootstrap file where LoggingInterceptor will be registered globally with app.useGlobalInterceptors(). Logger service already initialized and set as global logger. Reference for interceptor registration pattern.
- path: src/common/guards/jwt-auth.guard.ts kind: guard symbol: JwtAuthGuard reason: JWT authentication guard that runs before interceptors. LoggingInterceptor will see authenticated user context after this guard executes. Important for understanding request lifecycle execution order.
- path: src/common/guards/permissions.guard.ts kind: guard symbol: PermissionsGuard reason: Permissions guard that runs after JwtAuthGuard. LoggingInterceptor runs before guards (request phase) and after guards/controller (response phase). Important for understanding execution order.
artifacts.dependencies: node: - "@nestjs/common": "^11.0.1" (NestInterceptor, ExecutionContext, CallHandler, Injectable) - "rxjs": "^7.8.1" (Observable, tap operator for intercepting responses) - "uuid": "^13.0.0" (v4 for generating request IDs) - "winston": "^3.18.3" (already installed for LoggerService, Story 7.3) - "@types/uuid": "^10.0.0" (TypeScript types for uuid, devDependency)

story-context.constraints: - MUST implement NestJS NestInterceptor interface with intercept(context: ExecutionContext, next: CallHandler) method - MUST use RxJS tap() operator to intercept response stream without modifying it - MUST inject LoggerService via constructor dependency injection (from Story 7.3) - MUST use process.hrtime.bigint() for high-precision duration calculation (sub-millisecond accuracy) - MUST check for existing X-Request-ID header before generating new UUID (propagate if exists) - MUST set X-Request-ID response header for all requests (generated or propagated) - MUST include requestId in context object for all log entries (request and response) - MUST use conditional log levels: info (2xx/3xx), warn (4xx), error (5xx) - MUST NOT log request body by default (security risk - sensitive data) - MUST NOT log response body by default (security risk - sensitive data) - MUST register interceptor globally in main.ts with app.useGlobalInterceptors() - MUST ensure interceptor overhead is < 5ms per request (performance requirement) - SHOULD reuse SENSITIVE_FIELDS list from logger.config.ts if request body logging is added later - SHOULD reuse sanitizeContext() pattern from logger.config.ts for request body sanitization - MUST follow existing project patterns: Jest for testing, @Injectable() decorator, project-relative imports - MUST create tests in __tests__/ subdirectory following established pattern (src/common/interceptors/__tests__/) - MUST achieve 90%+ test coverage for LoggingInterceptor

tests.standards: Testing standards from Story 7.3 and project conventions: - Jest framework for unit and integration tests - Tests in __tests__/ subdirectory (src/common/interceptors/__tests__/) - 90%+ coverage target for LoggingInterceptor - Mock ExecutionContext, CallHandler, and LoggerService for unit tests - Integration tests with real HTTP requests to verify end-to-end behavior - E2E tests for full request/response lifecycle with various status codes - Performance tests to verify < 5ms overhead per request - Test file naming: logging.interceptor.spec.ts - Use descriptive test names following pattern: "should [expected behavior] when [condition]" - Arrange-Act-Assert pattern for test structure - Mock process.hrtime.bigint() for deterministic duration testing
tests.locations: - src/common/interceptors/__tests__/logging.interceptor.spec.ts (unit tests) - test/**/*.e2e-spec.ts (E2E tests for request/response logging)
tests.ideas:
Unit Tests (AC-7.4.1, 7.4.2, 7.4.3): - Test: interceptor implements NestInterceptor interface correctly - Test: request logged with method, URL, userAgent, requestId, timestamp when request starts - Test: X-Request-ID generated when not present in request headers (UUID v4 format) - Test: X-Request-ID propagated when present in request headers (existing value used) - Test: X-Request-ID added to response headers - Test: response logged with statusCode, duration, requestId, timestamp when request completes - Test: duration calculated accurately using process.hrtime.bigint() (mock for deterministic test) - Test: duration rounded to 2 decimal places
Unit Tests (AC-7.4.3 - Conditional Log Levels): - Test: log level is 'info' for 200 status code (success) - Test: log level is 'info' for 201 status code (created) - Test: log level is 'info' for 304 status code (not modified - 3xx) - Test: log level is 'warn' for 400 status code (bad request) - Test: log level is 'warn' for 404 status code (not found) - Test: log level is 'error' for 500 status code (internal server error) - Test: log level is 'error' for 503 status code (service unavailable)
Unit Tests (AC-7.4.4 - Sensitive Data): - Test: request body NOT logged by default (security best practice) - Test: response body NOT logged by default (security best practice) - Test: sensitive fields excluded if request body logging is enabled in future
Unit Tests (AC-7.4.6 - Request ID Correlation): - Test: same requestId present in both request and response log entries - Test: requestId stored in request object (request.requestId) for access in controllers
Integration Tests: - Test: interceptor registered globally and runs for all HTTP requests - Test: request and response logs appear in console (development mode) - Test: X-Request-ID header present in HTTP response - Test: request ID consistent across request/response log pairs (correlation) - Test: duration values realistic and positive
E2E Tests: - Test: full request lifecycle logged (request → guards → controller → response) - Test: multiple concurrent requests have unique request IDs (no collision) - Test: authenticated requests include userId in context (from JwtAuthGuard) - Test: error responses (4xx, 5xx) logged with correct log levels - Test: successful responses (2xx) logged with 'info' level - Test: interceptor doesn't interfere with @Public routes
Performance Tests: - Test: interceptor adds < 5ms overhead per request (measure actual latency) - Test: high-precision duration calculation (sub-millisecond accuracy) - Test: no performance degradation under load (100-1000 req/sec) - Test: memory usage stable (no memory leaks from interceptor)
